title: Refactor buildah bud for vendoring
body:
Projects like libpod would benefit greatly if we could vendor in buildah
rather than execing it.  This refactor moves some of the key parsing
functions that buds uses to pkg/parse so that the project vendoring
buildah can also benefit from them.

Signed-off-by: baude <bbaude@redhat.com>

comments.body:
This essentially bakes the choice of CLI library that we're currently using for the command into the exported library API.  That makes things harder for everyone if we ever want to switch what the command is using to parse its flags, unless we sign up to implement and maintain the logic for them, too.
The commit log message in the patch and description in the PR name the new package as `pkg/parse`, but the code currently adds `cli` and `parse`.
@nalind this is a first pass, im open to recommendations.

Moby has a pkg subdirectory for commonly used things (https://github.com/moby/moby/tree/master/pkg). Were you thinking the cli/common.go should be under a /pkg directory here @nalind?  That might be a nice landing spot for that.

@baude, good first pass.  I'd vote to create a pkg dir then a cli and parse dir under each and put the related code in there for each.  I think that's what @nalind is recommending, and I think it's a good throught. Travis CI is green, pretty slick!
Looks like the tests are a random registry issue that's not related.  Going to try to restart them.
bot's taking a vacation, going to close/repopen to reinit tests.
I've pulled the commit and tested using the baseline and authentication tests.  Both passed with flying colors.
